question: In Utopia, what adjective did the name of More's book give to the English language for an impracticable scheme?
evidence: we call an impracticable scheme Utopian

question: In Utopia, how many families is each Syphogrant set over?
evidence: The Syphogrants dwell in those that are set over thirty families

question: During Utopian worship, what plays while the people sing hymns to the honour of God?
evidence: sing hymns to the honour of God, some musical instruments playing all the while

question: In Leviathan, what does Hobbes call an endeavour that is directed away from something?
evidence: And when the Endeavour is fromward something, it is generally called AVERSION

question: According to Hobbes, obedience to which laws is the greatest worship of all?
evidence: Obedience to his Lawes (that is, in this case to the Lawes of Nature,) is the greatest worship of all

question: In Leviathan, what does Hobbes say the Heathens called their holy water?
evidence: The Heathens had also their Aqua Lustralis, that is to say, Holy Water

question: In The Wealth of Nations, are secrets in manufactures or secrets in trade capable of being kept longer?
evidence: Secrets in manufactures are capable of being longer kept than secrets in trade

question: How many inhabitants is France supposed to contain, according to Adam Smith?
evidence: France, besides, is supposed to contain 24,000,000 of inhabitants

question: In the London porter brewery, into how many barrels is a quarter of malt commonly brewed?
evidence: a quarter of malt is commonly brewed into more than two barrels and a-half

question: In 1525, what battle destroyed French rule in Italy and left Francis I a prisoner?
evidence: the battle of Pavia destroyed the French rule in Italy, and left Francis I a prisoner

question: In The Prince, what two animals does Machiavelli say a prince must imitate, one to know the snares and one to frighten the wolves?
evidence: it is necessary to be a fox to discover the snares and a lion to terrify the wolves

question: On what date did the Florentines commence the battle described near the end of The Prince?
evidence: On the morning of 10 June 1328, the Florentines commenced the battle

question: In Meditations, who taught Marcus Aurelius simplicity, reasonableness, gratitude, and a love of true liberty?
evidence: Apollonius taught him simplicity, reasonableness, gratitude, a love of true liberty

question: In Meditations, where does Marcus Aurelius say the fountain of all good is to be found?
evidence: Look within; within is the fountain of all good

question: In the letter near the end of Meditations, at what town did the writer break his journey, a mile or so from the highroad?
evidence: we broke our journey at Anagnia, a mile or so from the highroad

question: In the Second Treatise, whose authority does Locke oppose to those who say there were never any men in the state of nature?
evidence: I will not only oppose the authority of the judicious Hooker

question: According to Locke, what alone can make a man a member of a commonwealth?
evidence: his actually entering into it by positive engagement, and express promise and compact

question: In the Critique of Pure Reason, Kant says pure knowledge a priori is that with which no what element is mixed up?
evidence: Pure knowledge à priori is that with which no empirical element is mixed up

question: In which of his works did the philosopher try to prove that the soul cannot be annihilated because a simple being cannot cease to exist?
evidence: He endeavoured to prove in his Phaedo, that the soul cannot be annihilated

question: Near the end of the Critique, when a dogmatist advances how many arguments for a proposition, Kant says we may be sure not one is conclusive?
evidence: the dogmatist advances with ten arguments in favour of a proposition

question: In the introduction to The Republic, what is said to be the ordering principle of the three other virtues?
evidence: that one is the ordering principle of the three others

question: In The Republic, out of the question of the sons of whom is the remainder of the work developed?
evidence: The remainder of the Republic is developed out of the question of the sons of Ariston

question: In Locke's Essay Concerning Human Understanding, are ideas said to be naturally imprinted on the mind, given that they are not known to children and idiots?
evidence: Not on Mind naturally imprinted, because not known to Children

question: In the Essay, which two boundless ideas does Locke ask how the mind comes by, since objects fall so short of that largeness?
evidence: how we come by those BOUNDLESS IDEAS of eternity and immensity

question: In the Essay, Locke says mixed modes and relations are archetypes without patterns, and so cannot but be what?
evidence: cannot but be adequate, everything being so to itself

question: In Newton's Chronology, a priestess of which deity was brought into Greece and set up the Oracle at Dodona?
evidence: A Priestess of _Jupiter Ammon_, being brought by _Phoenician_ merchants into _Greece_

question: In Newton's Chronology, the maritime coast extending from Sidon to the border of Egypt bears what name?
evidence: Maritima ora quæ a Sidone ad Ægypti limitem extenditur, nomen habet Phoenices

question: In Newton's Chronology, how long did Artaxerxes Mnemon, the son of Darius, reign?
evidence: he Reigned forty six years, and died

question: In his Essays, what does Montaigne say a strong memory is commonly coupled with?
evidence: a strong memory is commonly coupled with infirm judgment

question: In the Essays, what does Montaigne say resemblance does not so much make one as difference makes?
evidence: Resemblance does not so much make one as difference makes another

question: In Thus Spake Zarathustra, what does Nietzsche say crieth unto heaven, rather than your sin?
evidence: it is your self-satisfaction that crieth unto heaven

question: In Zarathustra, of what is the rosy dawn said to be jealous, having come too early?
evidence: Jealous is she always of the glows of my morning-dream

question: In The Wealth of Nations, what does Smith say all demand for certain commodities may be stopped for, perhaps for a twelvemonth?
evidence: for which all demand is stopped for six months, perhaps for a twelvemonth

question: In Leviathan, Hobbes says leisure is the mother of philosophy, and what is the mother of peace and leisure?
evidence: Common-wealth, the mother of Peace, and Leasure

question: How many foot soldiers did Castruccio assemble, along with four thousand horsemen?
evidence: He assembled twenty thousand foot soldiers and four thousand horsemen

question: In Utopia, besides herbs, fruits, and bread, what else is sold at the markets for provisions?
evidence: there are not only herbs, fruits, and bread, but also fish, fowl, and cattle
